name: Foz – return via Ponte Vella of Rio Ouro
name_original: Foz – vuelta por Ponte Vella de río Ouro
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Foz are primarily characterized by their coastal proximity, offering paths along the Atlantic Ocean and the Ría de Foz estuary. The terrain typically features a mix of flat promenades, gentle inclines, and some more challenging sections with varied elevation. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse beaches, riverbanks, and the surrounding countryside. The region's landscape provides a blend of sea views and natural areas suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Foz The most popular touring cycling route is Foz – return via Ponte Vella of Rio Ouro, a moderate 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey along the Rio Ouro, featuring a historic bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ría de Foz – Altar Beach loop from Marzán, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.8 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the Ría de Foz estuary and includes the Altar Beach coastline. Local touring cyclists also love the Foz Promenade – Coastal Walkway of Foz loop from Foz, a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and walkways, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Foz is defined by coastal paths, scenic beaches, and river estuaries. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to more difficult routes with greater elevation gain. The routes in Foz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 39 reviews. More than 299 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Foz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Foz?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Foz, offering a diverse range of experiences from moderate coastal rides to more challenging routes with greater elevation gain. The komoot community has explored these routes over 299 times.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Foz?
A: While Foz offers many beautiful routes, the touring cycling trails are generally rated as moderate to difficult. There are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' in the current selection, but moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017395755/foz-return-via-ponte-vella-of-rio-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz – return via Ponte Vella of Rio Ouro&lt;/a&gt; (14.7 km) provide a good starting point for those looking for less strenuous rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Foz?
A: The terrain in Foz is primarily coastal, featuring paths along the Atlantic Ocean and the Ría de Foz estuary. You'll find a mix of flat promenades, gentle inclines, and some more challenging sections with varied elevation. Routes often traverse beaches, riverbanks, and the surrounding countryside, offering a blend of sea views and natural areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Foz?
A: Yes, Foz offers several excellent circular routes. For scenic views of the estuary and coastline, consider the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38190756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Foz – Altar Beach loop from Marzán&lt;/a&gt; (30.8 km). Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz Promenade – Coastal Walkway of Foz loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt; (24.7 km), which takes you along coastal promenades.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see along the cycling routes in Foz?
A: Foz is rich in natural beauty. Along your rides, you can encounter stunning coastal features like the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt;, known for its impressive rock formations. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Foz&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Castros Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer picturesque views where the Douro River meets the Atlantic Ocean.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Foz?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Foz has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rinlo 🌉🏘️ – Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Marzán&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 107.2 km ride with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz Promenade – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Nois&lt;/a&gt;, covering 49.4 km.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Foz?
A: The touring cycling routes in Foz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 39 reviews. Cyclists often praise the beautiful coastal scenery, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore both beaches and riverbanks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building that can be visited. The region also features charming architecture, from grand 19th-century villas to cobbled streets in areas like Foz Velha, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Foz?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, coastal regions like Foz generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and tourist crowds are thinner. Summer can be warm, but the coastal breeze often makes it enjoyable, especially for early morning or late afternoon rides.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through natural areas like forests?
A: Yes, some routes venture beyond the immediate coastline into natural areas. For instance, you might find sections that lead &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the eucalyptus forest&lt;/a&gt;, offering a different kind of scenic experience away from the beaches and promenades.

Q: Are there opportunities for family-friendly cycling in Foz?
A: While there are no routes specifically designated as 'easy' for very young children, the flatter sections of coastal promenades, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz Promenade – Coastal Walkway of Foz loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt;, could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

The Paseo Marítimo de Foz is a public space for recreation and promenade with incredible views of the Galician north Atlantic coast.

The high concentration of iodine in these waters also has healing properties, so much so that at the beginning of the 20th century, doctors in the area began to recommend baths for trauma injuries and skin problems.

Foz, a small fishing village and former whaling port at the mouth of the Foz estuary. The area is a unique natural habitat of salt marshes for one of the largest seabird colonies in northwestern Spain.
